title: Houses for Rent on Cleveland's West Side
metaTitle: Houses for Rent West Side of Cleveland, OH
metaDescription: Explore houses for rent on Cleveland's west side — neighborhoods, ZIP codes, transit, and rent ranges. Section 8-friendly homes. Book a showing today.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent west side cleveland
secondaryKeywords: ohio city tremont old brooklyn rentals
answerBox: Cleveland's west side spans neighborhoods like Ohio City, Tremont, Detroit-Shoreway, Old Brooklyn, and Cudell, generally west of the Cuyahoga River and served by the RTA Red Line. Rental houses here run roughly $700 to $1,500 a month depending on size and condition. We work with a smaller number of Section 8-friendly homes on the west side, concentrated around Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway and Old Brooklyn.

sections.h2: Renting a house on Cleveland's west side
body: Cleveland's west side covers the neighborhoods on the western bank of the Cuyahoga River, including Ohio City, Tremont, Detroit-Shoreway, Edgewater, Old Brooklyn, Stockyards, Clark-Fulton, Cudell, West Boulevard, and the West Park/Kamm's Corners area, among others — 14 of the city's 34 official Statistical Planning Areas sit on this side, per the City Planning Commission's neighborhood framework.

Our rental selection is concentrated on Cleveland's East and Southeast side, but we do work with a smaller set of voucher-friendly homes on the west side, largely around Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (ZIP 44102) and Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre (ZIP 44109). [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and tell us the west-side area you're interested in, and we'll let you know what's currently open.

sections.h2: West side neighborhoods and what's nearby
body: Each west-side neighborhood has its own mix of landmarks and commercial corridors. Ohio City is home to the historic West Side Market and sits directly on the RTA Red Line. Tremont centers on Lincoln Park with a concentrated restaurant district. Detroit-Shoreway contains the Gordon Square Arts District along Detroit Avenue. Edgewater borders Edgewater Park, part of the Cleveland Metroparks lakefront system. Old Brooklyn sits near the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo, and Clark-Fulton runs along the MetroHealth main campus corridor on W. 25th Street.

rows: West Side Market; RTA Red Line (Ohio City station)
Lake Erie shoreline near Detroit Ave

sections.h2: How much does a west-side rental house cost in Cleveland?
body: Citywide, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts Cleveland's median asking rent at $1,250/mo overall, with 2-bedroom units around $1,100 and 3-bedroom units around $1,350. Within our own west-side selection, rents range from about $700 to $1,500 a month depending on bedroom count, condition, and whether the unit is part of a duplex or a standalone house — broadly consistent with what we see citywide, since pricing reflects the home itself rather than which side of town it's on.

The RTA Red Line — the region's heavy-rail line connecting Cleveland Hopkins International Airport to downtown's Tower City and on to the east side — runs directly through the west side, with a stop at Ohio City. West-side neighborhoods are also served by an extensive GCRTA bus network, and I-90 and I-71 both cut through the area, giving drivers quick access to downtown and the western suburbs.

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers on Cleveland's west side
body: Every home we work with, on either side of the city, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready — that includes our west-side inventory around Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway and Old Brooklyn. Keep in mind Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and the City of Cleveland does not currently require landlords to accept vouchers, though we do on every home we work with. Learn more in our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/), or compare against our [houses for rent on the east side of Cleveland](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-east-side-cleveland/), where most of our current inventory is concentrated.

Interested in a specific west-side neighborhood? See our pages on [houses for rent in Ohio City](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-ohio-city-cleveland/), [houses for rent in Tremont](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-tremont-cleveland/), or [houses for rent in Old Brooklyn](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-old-brooklyn-cleveland/) for neighborhood-specific detail, or browse the [full houses-for-rent hub](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) for citywide options.

faq.q: What neighborhoods make up Cleveland's west side?
faq.a: The west side includes Ohio City, Tremont, Detroit-Shoreway, Edgewater, Old Brooklyn, Stockyards, Clark-Fulton, Cudell, West Boulevard, and the West Park/Kamm's Corners area, among others — 14 of the city's 34 official neighborhoods sit west of the Cuyahoga River.

faq.q: Do you have rental houses on Cleveland's west side?
faq.a: Yes, though our current selection is concentrated on the East and Southeast side. We work with a smaller number of Section 8-friendly homes on the west side, mainly around Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102) and Old Brooklyn (44109). Book a showing to check current availability.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house on Cleveland's west side?
faq.a: Rents generally run $700 to $1,500 a month depending on bedroom count and condition, in line with Zumper's July 4, 2026 citywide median of $1,250/mo. Pricing reflects the home's size and condition rather than the side of town.

faq.q: Is the RTA Red Line on Cleveland's west side?
faq.a: Yes. The Red Line runs from Cleveland Hopkins International Airport through the west side, including a stop in Ohio City, before continuing through downtown's Tower City hub to the east side.

faq.q: Do west-side rental houses accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Every home we work with, including our west-side homes, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Note that the City of Cleveland does not have a source-of-income protection ordinance, so acceptance varies by individual landlord.
